description: Fix GCP Terraform module Google provider constraints to allow 7.x+ consumers

userStories.title: Reproduce provider 7.x incompatibility in a failing check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a failing reproduction for Google provider 7.x resolution so that I can prove the constraint bug exists before fixing it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add/adjust a fixture or validation check that fails before the fix because module constraint '~> 6.0' blocks provider 7.x
The reproduction path uses realistic module consumption and required_providers configuration
The failing check/test name clearly indicates provider-constraint incompatibility
userStories.notes: Reproduction must be implemented before changing module constraints.

userStories.title: Update affected modules to open provider upper bound
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to change Google provider constraints in affected GCP modules so that consumers on 6.x and 7.x+ can use the modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Update required_providers.google.version from '~> 6.0' to '>= 6.0' in all five affected module main.tf files
No unrelated module functionality is changed
US-001 reproduction now passes after the constraint update
userStories.notes: Affected files: compute/trustgrid_single_node, network/trustgrid_mgmt_firewall, network/trustgrid_gateway_firewall, iam/trustgrid_node_service_account, iam/trustgrid_cluster_route_role.

userStories.title: Align documentation and add regression guardrails
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want docs and verification steps updated so the repository consistently documents GCP provider compatibility without an artificial upper bound.
acceptanceCriteria: Update documentation that currently states GCP uses '~> 6.0' to reflect '>= 6.0'
Regenerate README TF docs sections where provider requirements tables are stale
Run Terraform validation workflow in touched module/example directories
userStories.notes: Docs likely include AGENTS.md and GCP module README requirement/provider tables.
